voters.name: Caleb
voters.comment: Yeah, but... C'mon. We all know we like this song at this pointAlso, no. This was meant to be a 100% serious fire 'em up song. The song was written to be a sort of melancholy anthem for the fans that written to express how much their music had helped with their fans feeling like outcasts. Apparently, they shoved this one out at the last minute and had almost no time to record it. They had to bring in a studio drummer and recycle drum loops from previous songs in order to get it out in time. The half-assed sort of sloppy nature of the song was completely unintentional.

voters.name: Caleb
voters.comment: Yeah, but... C'mon. We all know we like this song at this pointAlso, no. This was meant to be a 100% serious fire 'em up song. The song was written to be a sort of melancholy anthem for the fans that written to express how much their music had helped with their fans feeling like outcasts. Apparently, they shoved this one out at the last minute and had almost no time to record it. They had to bring in a studio drummer and recycle drum loops from previous songs in order to get it out in time. The half-assed sort of sloppy nature of the song was completely unintentional.

The devil lets the singer know that it's fine to take time to yourself to take of yourself and not worry about others. Then, the singer throws it back on the devil when the devil complains about being overworked and too tired. The devil responds by saying that the singer wants everyone to need them or they would let it got or at least accept help. Finally, the singer resolves to just let everything go.

voters.name: Steve
voters.comment: Back when I worked at a summer tech camp, I traveled to locations all over the country. One spring session, me and my one coworker used to sneak up on each other and surprise each other by playing this song at full blast. No idea why but it became our own personal Rickroll. Summer started, he went to Florida, I went to California and a few months went by. The last week of camp, I was sitting quietly working on my laptop and all of a sudden, my internet browser started acting weird and any time I opened a new tab, it redirected to a YouTube video of this damn song. WTF! I sat there staring at my screen in disbelief as this dude who was supposed to be working on the other side of the country came running around the corner with the biggest shit eating grin on his face, saying nothing but the two words: "you're welcome." I've never been pranked so hard in my life and I hate that you're making me give points to this fucking song!!

voters.name: Amanda
voters.comment: My mom had our house exorcised and a cross put in the wall when I was a kid. Jess claimed she saw a lady in white robes and white hair standing at the foot of her bed. On later seeing photos of my mom's mom she said that was her in her hospital gowns after she was institutionalized. I never met her, she died before I was born of a heart attack and Jessica met her once or twice I think. I figure now Jess was just fucking with me like her and her childhood friend performing seances. But my dad's girlfriend now says she senses a presence all these years later. And my dad is asking me about the house and my experiences. We all had some odd experiences not all at that house after my Mom passed. Grayson's daycare provider said he  was talking to Nonnie but no one was there when Nicole called her. To say she would be late.My mom was en route to the ER at that momen.t I  attribute it complicated grief mental condition 😅  😆 So this is probably my Dad. We are probably those neighbors.

Apparently, the U and P in Super look like 9 11 when flipped backwards and is clearly a plane over NYC, and there's only ever been one time that planes were over NYC. The comments pointed out all the reasons this is ridiculous, though one comment on the original post claimed that the drink the waitress is holding means that the Jews did 9/11 (because I guess they're assuming it's juice?). Someone in the original post commented that they should listen to The Logical Song. To be fair, that's just good advice to listen to a good song.*Ahem* As a ska boy, I truly believe that more songs need brass instruments. This may be a tuba, which is very limited in it's use, but thumbs up.

voters.name: Caleb
voters.comment: I still think the best thing that P!atD did was to change their sound. This song is good and memorable, but there's no arguing that they only made it because of Fall Out Boy. When the other members left, or he kicked them out... I'm not sure what happened to them, and he changed to a much more pop rock sound, the music got much better and more unique. So much so that, when Fall Out Boy came back from their hiatus, they ripped off what he was doing at the time to be their new sound. Call me a hipster, but I prefer that run of Take This to Your Grave to Infinity On High over ANY song they've put out since.

voters.name: Amanda
voters.comment: Depends on the song but they do make sense on the whole. This song is about wanting someone so much you want to be part of them or one with someone. "Devour them" They use some more unique imagery but still "I want to share your mouthful | I want to do all the things your lungs do so well | I'm gonna bed into you like a cat beds into a bean bag | Turn you inside out and lick you like a crisp packet ...Devour Me | If you really think that you can really stomach me.  That Minpin line refers to the book The Minpins by Ronald Dahl suggesting a desire to be a small, hidden part of the other persons world. Also, circling back watermelon sugar high is about oral sex and the pleasure of the female orgasm using fruit to convey that metaphor (watch the music video of Harry Styles eating watermelon). Granted not the most original of a pop song content given Lollipop, sugar high, etc. Etc. but still. It's pretty common to imply sexual feelings and acts on the radio without being direct.

voters.name: Caleb
voters.comment: Fun story, this song is actually a cover. The original writer decided to toss his original lyrics and take it to Rob Thomas. Rob Thomas rewrote the melody and lyrics, giving it the name Smooth. Thomas took it to Santana, thinking he would be the best man to bring the song to life. Santana thought Thomas should do the vocals. So, while it's labeled as Santana featuring Rob Thomas, it's really Rob Thomas featuring Santana.
